meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no version control (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to implement robust analytics to track your app's evolution and solve version control gaps. Master Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your codebase is a ticking time bomb. By integrating event-based analytics, you create a trail of state changes and user interactions. This provides a 'functional version history,' allowing you to reconstruct app behavior and debug performance regressions, effectively bridging the gap while you master professional Git workflows.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first major project, but you lack a safety net. Every save feels like a gamble; every feature addition threatens to break the fragile equilibrium of your folder structure. You are flying blind, hoping the code works while lacking the granular visibility needed to understand how your app behaves in the wild.

But here is the engineering secret: visibility is the precursor to control. By implementing a professional analytics layer, you stop guessing and start measuring. This isn't just about tracking users; it's about building a telemetry system that acts as a heartbeat for your application, giving you the confidence to ship, iterate, and build a career-defining portfolio.

steps.title: Define Your Telemetry Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the critical user events that define success. This creates a blueprint for tracking state transitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for tracking user onboarding, feature interaction, and error states in a React application. Ensure the schema supports nested metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a schema.json file for event tracking based on my current project structure'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Set up a centralized hub to handle incoming events, ensuring your code remains modular and clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a singleton AnalyticsProvider class that handles event buffering and batching to minimize network requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create src/services/analytics.ts and export a singleton instance'

steps.title: Hook into Application Lifecycle
steps.description: Integrate trackers into your mounting and unmounting lifecycle to capture session duration and navigation patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom React hook 'useAnalytics' that automatically logs component mount and unmount events with timing data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Inject useAnalytics into all main routes in the App.tsx file'

steps.title: Implement Error Boundary Logging
steps.description: Turn crashes into actionable data points by capturing stack traces before the app loses state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an ErrorBoundary component that captures errors and sends them to the analytics provider before rendering a fallback UI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Wrap the root application component with the new ErrorBoundary'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Connect your event stream to a dashboard to see your application's health in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to transform raw JSON event logs into a summary report showing daily active users and top event frequencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a report.py script to parse log files and output a summary table'

pro_tips: Always log the 'previous state' alongside the 'new state' in your events to simulate a primitive version history.
Use high-cardinality metadata like user session IDs to trace specific bug reports back to individual user journeys.
Implement a 'debug mode' flag that prints events to the console, allowing you to verify tracking without hitting the server.
Never log sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your analytics payloads; hash your IDs instead.
Set up a 'heartbeat' event that fires every 30 seconds to track how long users stay idle versus active.
content.next_steps: After completing this, move your project into a GitHub repository. Use the analytics data you've gathered to write a 'Post-Mortem' case study for your portfolio, showing potential employers how you track and optimize app performance.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical maturity of a junior engineer who understands the value of observability. Don't let your code drift into obscurity—start measuring your success today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
